notes: PT chart values are saturation pressures (psig) at the listed saturation temperatures (°F).
Operating pressure ranges are typical starting expectations only; always convert pressure to saturation temperature and compare to line temps to calculate superheat/subcooling.
For zeotropic blends, use dew point for vapor/suction side and bubble point for liquid/subcooling side unless the manufacturer specifies otherwise. (R-454B is a zeotropic blend.)

normal_operating_pressure_ranges.context: typical comfort cooling, properly charged, clean coils, adequate airflow
notes: Ranges shift with indoor load and outdoor ambient; use saturation temps + SH/SC for decisions.

normal_operating_pressure_ranges.context: typical comfort cooling, properly charged, clean coils, adequate airflow
notes: R-22 systems vary widely; use SH/SC and manufacturer charging method first.

normal_operating_pressure_ranges.context: typical comfort cooling, properly charged, clean coils, adequate airflow
notes: R-32 is an A2L refrigerant; follow A2L service safety procedures and local codes.

pt_chart.bubble_dew_notes: This dataset is a single vapor pressure table; for detailed glide handling, use manufacturer PT chart with bubble/dew columns when available.

normal_operating_pressure_ranges.context: typical comfort cooling, properly charged, clean coils, adequate airflow
notes: R-454B is A2L and a blend; charge by weight when required and verify with SH/SC per equipment data plate.
Use the equipment charging label when available. Many systems specify a target subcooling at a given outdoor temperature.
If subcool is high, suspect overcharge or a restriction in the liquid line; if low, suspect undercharge or flash gas.
Superheat targets depend on indoor wet-bulb and outdoor dry-bulb; use a superheat chart when possible.
If superheat is high, suspect undercharge, a restriction, or low airflow; if low, suspect overfeeding or low load.
Many heat pumps are charged in cooling mode using subcooling (TXV/EEV) or superheat (fixed orifice), then verified in heating mode per manufacturer.
Some heat pumps have different metering devices in heating vs cooling; follow the unit's charging chart and defrost-board instructions.
If conditions prevent running cooling mode (cold weather), charge by weight (weigh-in) plus manufacturer heating checks.

typical_checks: Verify indoor airflow and outdoor coil cleanliness first
Confirm reversing valve position and auxiliary heat status
Compare pressures to PT chart to get saturation temps, then check line temps

rules_of_thumb.use_pressure_as_temperature: Always convert measured pressure to saturation temperature using the PT chart before interpreting readings.
measurements_needed: Indoor return air dry-bulb and wet-bulb (or at least return DB + supply DB split)
Low-side pressure and suction line temperature (to calculate superheat)
High-side pressure and liquid line temperature (to calculate subcooling)

fault_signatures.name: Undercharge / leak
quick_checks: Look for oil stains at joints/service valves
Check Schrader cores and caps
Leak check and repair before recharging
fixes: Weigh in charge per nameplate, then fine-tune per SH/SC method

fault_signatures.name: Overcharge
likely_causes: Condenser airflow issues masquerading as overcharge
quick_checks: Confirm condenser fan operation
Confirm coil is clean
Confirm proper airflow before recovering charge

fault_signatures.name: Liquid line restriction (filter-drier, kink, partially closed valve)
quick_checks: Check for a temperature drop across filter-drier
Inspect for kinks/crimps
Verify valves fully open
fixes: Evacuate and weigh-in recharge if opened

fault_signatures.name: Low airflow across evaporator
quick_checks: Check filter and blower speed
Check coil cleanliness
Check static pressure if available
fixes: Re-check SH/SC after airflow is corrected

high_subcooling.definition: Subcooling above target (TXV systems) after the system is stabilized
likely_causes: Condenser airflow reduced (dirty coil, failed fan, blocked airflow)
Non-condensables in system (rare but possible after improper evacuation)
recommended_checks: Verify condenser fan operation and coil cleanliness
Measure temperature drop across filter-drier (restriction indicator)
Confirm metering device type and target subcool from data plate
Confirm outdoor ambient and allow stabilization time
fixes: If airflow issue: correct airflow first, then re-check
If restriction: replace restricted component and evacuate/recharge as needed
If overcharge: recover refrigerant to target subcool

low_subcooling.definition: Subcooling below target (TXV systems) after the system is stabilized
likely_causes: Flash gas in liquid line (long line set without proper charge adjustment, high ambient)
Metering device overfeeding (rare), or very low load
recommended_checks: Look for leak indicators (oil residue, bubbling at joints)
Confirm system has correct charge for line set length
Confirm indoor load and airflow are normal
fixes: If leak suspected: repair leak, then weigh-in charge
Add refrigerant per charging method until target subcool is reached (after stabilization)

high_superheat.definition: Superheat above target after stabilization (fixed orifice), or higher than expected (TXV)
likely_causes: Restricted metering device or liquid line
Low indoor airflow or low load (can raise SH in some cases)
TXV stuck closed / sensing bulb issue
recommended_checks: Check subcooling (helps separate undercharge vs restriction)
For TXV: inspect bulb mounting/insulation and equalizer line
fixes: If undercharged: repair leak and recharge
If restriction/TXV issue: repair/replace component and recharge if opened
If airflow issue: correct airflow first, then re-check

low_superheat.definition: Superheat below target (near 0°F indicates flooding risk)
likely_causes: Overcharge (more common on fixed orifice systems)
Low load (very cool indoor return air, low airflow in certain cases)
recommended_checks: Check subcooling (high SC supports overcharge/restriction patterns)
Listen for compressor flooding indicators and check accumulator (heat pumps)
fixes: If TXV overfeeding: correct bulb placement/insulation or replace TXV
If low load: normalize conditions and re-check

problem_catalog.name: Low airflow (evaporator)
problem_catalog.summary: Not enough air across indoor coil, leading to low coil temp, freezing, poor capacity.
common_indicators: High temp drop across coil or weak airflow

problem_catalog.name: Low airflow (condenser)
problem_catalog.summary: Outdoor coil can't reject heat, driving head pressure up.

problem_catalog.name: Undercharge / leak
problem_catalog.summary: Not enough refrigerant mass flow, reducing capacity and raising superheat.
fixes: Weigh in charge then verify by SH/SC

problem_catalog.name: Overcharge
problem_catalog.summary: Too much refrigerant, higher subcooling, risk of flooding and high head.

problem_catalog.name: Liquid line restriction
problem_catalog.summary: Restriction reduces refrigerant flow, causing high SC before the restriction and starving the evaporator.

problem_catalog.name: Metering device restriction / TXV stuck closed
problem_catalog.summary: Metering device limits flow too much, starving evaporator.

problem_catalog.name: Evaporator icing
problem_catalog.summary: Indoor coil freezes, choking airflow and reducing capacity.
common_indicators: Ice on suction line or coil
fixes: Fix airflow or charge issue causing icing

problem_catalog.name: Short cycling (controls/safeties)
problem_catalog.summary: Unit cycles off quickly due to thermostat, pressure switches, or protection logic.

quick_checks: Confirm thermostat set to COOL and indoor fan is running
Check filter and return/supply airflow
Check if outdoor unit is running and condenser fan spinning
Measure SH/SC to separate airflow vs charge vs restriction
Check thermostat placement and setpoints (large overshoot, direct sunlight, etc.)
Check high-pressure and low-pressure switch trip history if available
Inspect condenser coil and fan
Measure head pressure trend during run cycle
Check filter and blower operation
Verify indoor coil is not blocked and condensate is draining
Measure superheat (often high if undercharged, low/normal if airflow issue)
Let coil thaw before final readings
Check coil cleanliness (indoor + outdoor) and filters
Confirm system isn't running continuously due to low capacity
Measure temperature split and SH/SC
Look for duct leaks or return air issues
